title: First Non-Blank
intro: `0` goes to column 0, but in indented code you usually want `^` — the first non-blank character. `+` and `-` do the same while moving: straight to where the next or previous line's code begins.
summary: Land where the code starts, not column 0.

exercises.instruction: From the end of the line, `^` jumps to the first character of code, skipping the indent.
hints: `0` would land on the margin; `^` lands on the `r`.

exercises.instruction: `+` moves down to the first non-blank of the next line.
hints: One key: down a line AND onto the code.

exercises.instruction: `-` moves up, landing on the code, not the margin.
Delete from the cursor back to the start of the code with `d^` — the indent survives.
hints: `d^` deletes back to the first non-blank, not to column 0.

exercises.instruction: Walk down the stairs: press `+` twice to land on `three`.
hints: Each `+` follows the indentation for you.

exercises.instruction: Clean up: from `value`, `d^` removes the junk but keeps the indent.
hints: The deletion stops at the first non-blank character.
